title: Dengue genotyping by E gene amplification and sequencing
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"7le2m","text":"1)The partial E gene of dengue virus is amplified before sequencing by using four sets of serotype-specific 
   oligonucleotides as referenced in the manuscript text.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5n6ut","text":"2) The reaction mixture is prepared as the following:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ce70","text":"    12.5ul of MyTaq RT-PCR (Bioline, Korea)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67tua","text":"      4.5ul of  Nuclease-Free Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fgujc","text":"      1.0 ul of Foward Primer for the respective serotype  (10uM)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3bbpo","text":"      1.0 ul of Reverse Primer for the respective serotype (10uM)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4gno","text":"      0.5ul of RNase inhibitor","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"63j71","text":"      0.5ul of Reverse Transcriptase (RT)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7m7tg","text":" 3) Aliquot 20ul in each PCR tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"130o4","text":" 4) 5ul of Dengue RNA (with known serotype) is added to the corresponding PCR tube.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ah3p9","text":" 5) PCR amplification is performed on CFX-96 (BioRad) conventional PCR thermal cycler.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ehala","text":" 6) The PCR is performed with the following cycling profile: 35 cycles (94 °C for 1 min, 55 °C for 1 min and 72 °C for 1.5 min) followed by an extension reaction at        72 °C for 5 min.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f7q7o","text":" 7) A 25 µl aliquot of each PCR reaction is analyzed on 1.5 % pre-stained agarose by gel electrophoresis, run for 30 min at 90 V.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"igv8","text":" 8) The gel is then viewed under UV illumination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9jm4h","text":" 9) The expected amplicon size for each serotype is 578bp for DENV1, 617bp for DENV 2, 582bp for DENV3 and 572bp for DENV 4 The corresponding 
     amplicons are extracted from the agarose gel and purified by Gel Extraction Kit (Qiagen, USA) according to the manufacturer ’s instruction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2u9ng","text":" 10) The final elution volume is 30 µl of purified PCR amplicons. Then, 5 µl of these are reanalyzed on 1.5 % agarose gel to substantiate the accuracy of 
      purification step. The purified PCR amplicons were outsourced for sequencing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Jeyanthi Suppiah
